elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_0
elements.text: The sink and swim methods were inspired by the book Algorithms by Sedgewick and Wayne, Fourth Edition, Section 2.4.

elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_1
elements.text: The structure accepts any type that conforms to the Compar ab le protocol. The single initializer allows you to optionally specify the sorting order and a list of starting values. The default sorting order is descending and the default starting values are an empty collection:

elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_2
mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_3
elements.text: /// Initializing with starting values priorityQueue = PriorityQueue<String>(ascending: true, startingvalues: ["Coldplay", "oneRepublic", "Maroon 5","Imagine Dragons", "The script"])

elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_4
elements.text: var x = priorityQueue.pop() /// coldplay x = priorityQueue.pop() /// Imagine Dragons

page: book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111
primary_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_5

elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_5
mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_6
elements.text: The PriorityQueue conforms to sequence, collection, and IteratorProt ocol, so you can treat it like any other Swift sequence and collection:

elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_7
elements.text: extension PriorityQueue: IteratorProtocol { public typealias Element = T mutating public func next() -> Element? { return pop() } } extension PriorityQueue: Sequence { public typealias Iterator = PriorityQueue public func makeIterator() -> Iterator { return self } }

elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_8
elements.text: This allows you to use Swift standard library functions on a PriorityQueue and iterate through a PriorityQueue like this:

elements.id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_9
elements.text: for x in priorityQueue { print(x) } // coldplay
